problems.title: Save Data with Duck Typing
problems.description: Create two saver classes that both expose a `save(data)` method. Inside main(), write a helper that accepts any saver object, then call it once with a file saver and once with a console saver to demonstrate duck typing. Implement the logic in main(...) and match the expected output for the provided cases.
problems.prompt: Create two saver classes that both expose a `save(data)` method. Inside main(), write a helper that accepts any saver object, then call it once with a file saver and once with a console saver to demonstrate duck typing.

examples.input:
Use the sample setup:
file_saver = FileSaver()
console_saver = ConsoleSaver()
store_data(file_saver, 'Data to save')
store_data(console_saver, 'Data to save')
examples.output:
Saved to file: Data to save
Data to save

test_cases.expected_output:
Saved to file: Data to save
Data to save
test_cases.label: Test 1

problems.title: Compose with Any Writer
problems.description: Create two classes, `Pen` and `Keyboard`, that both implement `write()`. Inside main(), pass each one into a shared `compose()` function and print the output from both objects. Implement the logic in main(...) and match the expected output for the provided cases.
problems.prompt: Create two classes, `Pen` and `Keyboard`, that both implement `write()`. Inside main(), pass each one into a shared `compose()` function and print the output from both objects.

examples.input:
Use the sample setup:
pen = Pen()
keyboard = Keyboard()
compose(pen)
compose(keyboard)
examples.output:
Writing with a pen.
Typing with a keyboard.

test_cases.expected_output:
Writing with a pen.
Typing with a keyboard.
test_cases.label: Test 1

problems.title: Runtime Type Checking with hasattr
problems.description: Create a `Dog` class with `bark()` and a `pet_speak()` helper that checks for `bark()` using `hasattr`. Inside main(), call it once with a dog and once with a string to show both branches. Implement the logic in main(...) and match the expected output for the provided cases.
problems.prompt: Create a `Dog` class with `bark()` and a `pet_speak()` helper that checks for `bark()` using `hasattr`. Inside main(), call it once with a dog and once with a string to show both branches.

examples.input:
Use the sample setup:
dog = Dog()
pet_speak(dog)
pet_speak("I'm not a pet")

problems.title: Error Potential When Methods Are Missing
problems.description: Create a `Car` class with `drive()` and a `start_trip()` helper that expects any object passed to it to implement `drive()`. Inside main(), show the successful car call and then catch and print the `AttributeError` raised by passing a plain string. Implement the logic in main(...) and match the expected output for the provided cases.
problems.prompt: Create a `Car` class with `drive()` and a `start_trip()` helper that expects any object passed to it to implement `drive()`. Inside main(), show the successful car call and then catch and print the `AttributeError` raised by passing a plain string.

examples.input:
Use the sample setup:
car = Car()
start_trip(car)
bicycle = "I'm a bicycle"
examples.output:
Car is driving.
AttributeError: 'str' object has no attribute 'drive'

test_cases.expected_output:
Car is driving.
AttributeError: 'str' object has no attribute 'drive'
test_cases.label: Test 1

problems.title: hasattr and getattr in Practice
problems.description: Create a `Calculator` class with a `calculate()` method and a helper that uses `hasattr()` and `getattr()` to call an operation by name. Inside main(), demonstrate one supported operation and one unsupported one. Implement the logic in main(...) and match the expected output for the provided cases.
problems.prompt: Create a `Calculator` class with a `calculate()` method and a helper that uses `hasattr()` and `getattr()` to call an operation by name. Inside main(), demonstrate one supported operation and one unsupported one.

examples.input:
Use the sample setup:
calc = Calculator()
perform_operation(calc, 'calculate')
perform_operation(calc, 'print')
examples.output:
Calculating result.
Operation print not supported.

test_cases.expected_output:
Calculating result.
Operation print not supported.
test_cases.label: Test 1
